market_summary: REGIME: MIXED/DEFENSIVE — SPY 1m=-5.67%, below BOTH MA50 and MA200 (first MA200 breach in this run series), QQQ 1m=-4.39% also below both MAs, IWM 1m=-8.30% below MA50 but above MA200. SPY MA200 breach is a material regime deterioration vs prior sessions where SPY held MA200 support. TLT 1m=-3.69%, below both MAs — bond bid fully absent, stagflation/rates-pressure macro regime unchanged. BTC-USD mom=-23.84%, 3m=-27.54%, well below MA200 — crypto fully risk-off. GLD mom=+15.26%, 3m=+3.72%, above MA200 but now -10.2% below MA50 (close=$413.38 vs MA50=$456.13) — gold in sharp pullback but structural 12m=+47.24% thesis intact. Portfolio weighted avg momentum ~30% vs SPY mom=+1.35% — +29pp alpha spread maintained despite broad equity distribution. Regime: MIXED/DEFENSIVE with elevated tail risk (SPY MA200 breach).

LEADERSHIP: All 8 holdings retain positive momentum composites. XOM leads quality/momentum: mom=+33.14%, 3m=+37.92%, above BOTH MA50 and MA200, dd63=-5.48%, vol20=18.74% — cleanest holding, no flags. CVX: mom=+26.85%, 3m=+37.93%, above BOTH MAs, vol20=18.90%, dd63=-5.28% — lowest risk, no flags. KLAC: mom=+44.39% (portfolio leader), 3m=+22.76%, above BOTH MAs (close=$1,498.67 vs MA50=$1,472.37), dd63=-22.41%, vol20=46.95% — strong 12m=+110.76%, Q1-FY2026 EPS=$9.14 ramping to $12.10 by Q4-FY2026. LMT: mom=+25.61%, 3m=+34.16%, above BOTH MAs (close=$627.43 vs MA50=$625.03), dd63=-7.28% — defense rearmament thesis intact, Q1 EPS=$6.72 ramping to $8.45 by Q4. TSM: mom=+29.47%, 3m=+15.98%, below MA50 ($346.68 vs close=$329.24, gap=-4.9%) but above MA200=$283.83, dd63=-14.85%, 1m=-10.90% — tariff/geopolitical overhang persists; EPS ramp Q1=$3.26→Q4=$3.99. GOOGL: mom=+24.77%, below MA50 ($317.31) but above MA200 ($260.22), 1m=-4.37%, 3m=-0.41%, EPS Q1=$2.76→Q4=$3.21. SLV: mom=+35.82%, 1m=-19.71%, dd63=-41.74%, vol20=54.84% — severe near-term drawdown, close=$61.52 vs MA200=$51.57 (buffer=+19.3%) still above kill-switch floor but thesis damage flag active. GLD: mom=+15.26%, 1m=-11.79%, dd63=-16.64%, close=$413.38 vs MA200=$374.65 (buffer=+10.3%) — below MA50 for second consecutive session, 29% allocation is the primary concentration risk in current regime.

GO/NO-GO: NON-REBALANCE DAY — Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 5 (minimum cadence = 7 days; next eligible window = 2026-03-25). SPY MA200 breach is a significant deterioration flag, but no individual holding has breached an emergency kill-switch threshold: SLV close=$61.52 vs MA200=$51.57 buffer=+19.3%; GLD close=$413.38 vs MA200=$374.65 buffer=+10.3%; TSM close=$329.24 vs MA200=$283.83 buffer=+16.0%; GOOGL close=$301.00 vs MA200=$260.22 buffer=+15.7%. All equity EPS acceleration trajectories confirmed intact via qualitative refresh (XOM Q1=$1.65→Q3=$2.00, CVX Q1=$1.69→Q2=$2.12, KLAC Q1=$9.14→Q4=$12.10, LMT Q1=$6.72→Q4=$8.45, GOOGL Q1=$2.76→Q4=$3.21, TSM Q1=$3.26→Q4=$3.99). Pre-logging for 2026-03-25 rebalance: (1) SLV dd63=-41.74% most severe in portfolio — trim/eliminate evaluation required; (2) GLD 29% weight with 1m=-11.79% — sizing review required; (3) SPY MA200 breach signals potential regime shift that may require defensive repositioning. DO NOTHING — hold portfolio exactly unchanged.

thesis: NON-REBALANCE DAY: Rebalance due = false. Days since last rebalance checkpoint = 5; minimum cadence = 7 days. Next eligible rebalance window = 2026-03-25. No emergency kill-switch threshold has been breached across any of the 8 holdings. Portfolio weighted momentum composite ~30% vs SPY mom=+1.35% — +29pp alpha spread is the strongest signal that current positioning is working despite the mixed/defensive regime. SPY MA200 breach (close=$648.57 vs MA200=$656.82) is a new deterioration event that will inform the 2026-03-25 rebalance evaluation but does not trigger an emergency override today.

FACTOR EDGE: KLAC (mom=+44.39%) remains above both MA50 and MA200 with FY2026 EPS ramp $9.14→$12.10 — cleanest quality/momentum name in the portfolio. XOM and CVX both above both MAs with near-identical 3m returns (+37.92%/+37.93%) and minimal drawdowns (-5.48%/-5.28%) — energy cluster is the portfolio's most robust factor exposure. LMT above both MAs with 3m=+34.16%, dd63=-7.28% — defense rearmament thesis is intact and the least volatile equity in the macro hedge bucket. The combined GLD (29%) + SLV (4%) = 33% precious metals allocation now shows 1m=-11.79%/-19.71% acute drawdown, but 12m=+47.24%/+101.70% structural momentum and MA200 buffers of +10.3%/+19.3% remain above kill-switch thresholds. Pre-committing to SLV/GLD sizing review at 2026-03-25 rebalance.

VARIANT VIEW: Holding unchanged rather than emergency-trimming GLD or SLV: the SPY MA200 breach and simultaneous precious metals drawdown is consistent with a risk-off margin call / tariff-driven dollar-strengthening episode rather than a fundamental reversal of the commodity bull thesis. In prior acute liquidation events (analogous to 2022 March, 2020 March), gold's MA200 acted as structural support and prices recovered within 3-4 weeks once the forced selling cleared. The bear case — sustained precious metals bear market — requires a genuine hawkish policy pivot or dollar structural rally, neither of which is evidenced by TLT (still declining, not rising) or by any of the 6 equity EPS acceleration trajectories being impaired. At 2026-03-25 rebalance, if GLD and SLV have not begun recovering toward MA50, reduction of GLD from 29% toward 22-25% will be evaluated.
risks: MACRO: SPY MA200 breach (close=$648.57 vs MA200=$656.82, gap=-1.3%) is a new regime deterioration flag — if sustained for 3+ sessions, increases probability of broader equity de-risking that could extend precious metals liquidation further. TLT below both MAs removes the flight-to-safety bond bid. Combined GLD+SLV = 33% weight means a further -10% move in both precious metals would cost the fund ~330bps of NAV. Tariff escalation risk (April 2 reciprocal tariff deadline) is the primary binary event that could accelerate TSM (-5% below MA50) and tech sector weakness.

IDIOSYNCRATIC: SLV dd63=-41.74% and vol20=54.84% — a 1-sigma daily move is ~3.5%; the MA200 buffer of +19.3% could be consumed within ~5-6 adverse sessions if drawdown accelerates. GLD buffer to MA200 is only +10.3% (close=$413.38 vs MA200=$374.65) — tighter than prior sessions. KLAC vol20=46.95% with dd63=-22.41% — any semis sector risk-off rotation could breach MA50 support despite strong 12m momentum. TSM 1m=-10.90% persistent MA50 breach creates risk of further deterioration if tariff announcements target Taiwan-origin semiconductor imports.

EXECUTION/LIQUIDITY: No execution actions today. All 8 holdings are highly liquid. Monitoring checkpoints pre-committed for 2026-03-25 rebalance: (1) SLV close vs MA200 $51.57 — emergency exit if breached; (2) GLD recovery above MA50 $456.13 required within 14 days or weight reduction from 29% to 22-25% at next rebalance; (3) SPY — if MA200 breach is confirmed for 3+ consecutive sessions, regime shifts to RISK-OFF and defensive repositioning is mandatory at 2026-03-25; (4) TSM must show 1m momentum recovery toward 0% by 2026-03-25 or trim is evaluated.
falsifiable_checks: SLV KILL-SWITCH: If SLV closes below MA200 $51.57 at any point before 2026-03-25 rebalance, emergency exit review is triggered regardless of schedule. Current buffer: $61.52 vs $51.57 = +19.3%. SLV must show recovery above MA50 $78.01 within 21 days (by 2026-04-13) or position is reduced from 4% to 2% at the next rebalance. Secondary check: if SLV 1w return deteriorates below -20% (currently -15.98%), pre-rebalance emergency review is triggered on 2026-03-24.

GLD SIZING TRIGGER: GLD close=$413.38 vs MA200=$374.65 — buffer=+10.3%. If GLD fails to recover above MA50 $456.13 by 2026-03-25 AND SPY remains below MA200 for 3+ sessions, GLD weight is reduced from 29% to 22-25% at 2026-03-25 rebalance (freeing 4-7% for energy or defense additions). SPY MA200 BREACH MONITOR: SPY close=$648.57 vs MA200=$656.82 — if this breach persists through 2026-03-25, regime classification upgrades to RISK-OFF and the 2026-03-25 rebalance becomes a mandatory defensive repositioning event.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base case: Do nothing. This is a non-rebalance day (day 5 of 7-day minimum cadence). No holding has crossed an emergency kill-switch threshold. The portfolio's +29pp alpha spread over SPY is the primary signal that current positioning is outperforming the market. Bull case: The SPY MA200 breach is temporary (tariff-driven panic, not fundamental), GLD and SLV stabilize above MA200 supports, KLAC and XOM continue to lead, and the fund's defensive/commodity/quality-tech mix captures the next leg of the rotation into 2026-03-25 rebalance. Bear case: SPY MA200 breach is sustained, GLD and SLV continue declining toward MA200 floors, tariff escalation hits TSM and KLAC hard, and the 33% precious metals allocation becomes a significant drag. Bear case probability ~30% — elevated vs prior sessions due to SPY MA200 breach, but all 8 holdings still have positive momentum composites and MA200 buffers above emergency thresholds. Expected edge of doing nothing vs emergency trading: saves ~30-50bps round-trip costs, avoids selling at potential panic lows in GLD/SLV, and preserves the formal rebalance evaluation framework for 2026-03-25 where a full scoring review can be conducted with 2 more sessions of price data.
